summary.rationale: The workflow now captures browser evidence and records visual constraints structurally, which makes design refinement more defensible and repeatable.

findings.message: Older runs created before preview evidence became required will not validate against the new design-review contract until they are refreshed or excluded from active validation.
findings.recommendation: Backfill preview_evidence for important historical runs or document that only active runs are expected to meet the new contract.
evidence: The new schema requires preview_evidence with at least one entry.
Historical runs were created before this field existed.

notes: Design review evidence is now a mechanically enforced requirement.
Visual constraints are now required by the design artifact schema.
Common repeated workflow steps now have deterministic wrappers.
The refine command makes iteration state easier to follow.
The workflow remains lightweight and additive rather than over-orchestrated.
The updated contracts reduce ambiguity without adding excessive ceremony.
The workflow improvements prefer quiet, structured constraints over bulky new systems.
The design evidence requirement is more concrete than the previous prose-only rule.

next_steps: Consider adding optional migration helpers for historical runs if old artifacts need to be revalidated frequently.
If screenshot evidence also needs to be visible in verify outputs later, add a mirrored field there instead of duplicating ad hoc notes.
